items.caption_text:
Half my face aged in 3 weeks.
I’m a board-certified derm, and I quit every product on the left side of my face, except sunscreen, for 21 days.

The right side? My full routine.

The result: Visible photo damage reappears: sun spots + uneven tone. That is what aging looks like in real time, and that’s exactly the kind of damage a real skincare routine can prevent 👏

To everyone who says you can’t age this fast: skin doesn’t lie.

💬 Comment EVEN and I’ll DM you the exact routine I used on the right side.

Full methodology: same sleep, same diet, no driving (I’m in NYC, no car), SPF on both sides daily.

items.caption_text:
Arabic isn’t scary. It’s not a headline. It’s the language I grew up hearing: my family laughing in it, cooking in it, telling me they love me in it.

If it sounds “intense” to you (I’m being polite… IYKYK), that’s a media problem, not a language problem.

So I remade this reel in Arabic, as a derm explaining why your face starts sagging in your 30s.

Comment ❤️ if you want more skincare in Arabic… (clearly I need the practice 😂)

items.caption_text:
PSA: This is your sign to get your mammogram. I just got mine.

📊 1 in 8 women will get breast cancer. Caught early, ~99% survive.

🫣 And yet, so many still don’t go. Fear, avoidance, “later”… I get it. But early detection is everything.
👩‍⚕️ Start at 40 or earlier if high risk (talk to your doctor)

Ultrasound? Not a replacement, but can be added for dense breasts or specific concerns. (I do both.)

Go get your scan.

👇 Have you had yours? What was your experience? Any advice or questions?

items.caption_text:
Imagine being so triggered by… another language.

Thank you @annehathaway for spotlighting the word “Inshallah,” an Arabic word that transcends borders, cultures and religions and that signifies “god willing”, or culturally “hopefully” especially during Arab American Heritage Month 🥹

Growing up, I used to soften parts of my Arab identity because of how Arabs were portrayed - portrayal being the key word. Not because I wasn’t proud… but because it felt easier. Especially when a former employer once introduced me as being “from the French part of Washington DC” when patients asked about my accent 🤯

So if you’ve ever felt like you had to shrink parts of who you are to fit in: I see you ❤️ Maybe it’s time we unlearn that. Together. AMEEN 🙌

items.caption_text:
If your skin feels ‘off’ after Botox… you’re not imagining it. 👀 This isn’t just about freezing muscles. There’s more going on beneath the surface. Allow me, a board-certified cosmetic dermatologist, to break it down for you. @emmaclareannehill 

Working out after Botox isn’t the issue here—at two months post-treatment, exercise has no impact on your results.

Because 99% of the comments are wrong on your video, I hope this helps shed some light ❤️

items.caption_text:
Comment SAFE and I’ll send you links to the products from the @sephora sale that I would use if I were pregnant🤰 
As a woman who went through IVF multiple times to have both of my babies and as a dermatologist, I know how stressful this phase can be 🤯 You want to feel like yourself, take care of your skin, and look your best… even when you don’t feel your best.

These are the ingredients and products I personally would feel comfortable using if I were pregnant.

If you want me to go deeper: routines, what to avoid, what actually matters—just let me know below!

items.caption_text:
With the weight of everything happening right now, it feels strange to celebrate… but I’m allowing myself this small moment: two years at Sephora, and hope for what’s ahead 🙏

Pic 1: Today. Celebrating 2 years of @dr.idriss @sephora 
Pic 2: None of this could have ever been possible without all of you and as luck would have it - as I was standing for this picture a fellow nerd @vanearellanor came up to me and let me know how much our PillowtalkDerm family has helped her over the years 🥹 the significance of the timing was not lost me 
Pic 3: Forever grateful and honored to be a part of this journey alongside each one of you. I’ll be popping into Sephoras across NYC all throughout the sale (4/10-4/20). If you see me please say hi 💜
Pic 4: 2 years ago for our launch @sephora 
Pic 5: 1 year ago
Pic 6: 4 years ago raw footage of me balling my eyes out of fear, emotion and disbelief 2 min prior to launching our brand
Pic 7: … and back to work we go 😎

Never in my wildest dreams did I think my small, self-funded brand, built by an Arab woman with zero business background and zero investors, would have a place at all - let alone on shelves @sephora. Period. 

And this year’s picture and the moment captured behind it—might just be my favorite yet. Because it was never about me. It was always about YOU. Your trust. Your belief. Your support.

So, if you’re shopping the Sephora sale, and spot @dr.idriss in stores, please feel free to take a selfie and share with us because we’re celebrating with you, always.

From the bottom of my heart: THANK YOU!!!!!
Let’s keep building. Together. 🚀💜🔥

items.caption_text:
For years, I’ve donated to Doctors Without Borders to help people in crisis around the world.

I never imagined I’d be doing it for my own country.

This fundraiser is for the people who  have been hit the hardest. Innocent lives who need care, safety, and support right now.

It may not bring peace.
But if it can bring help, then it’s worth everything.

If you’re able to contribute, thank you. 🤍🇱🇧

items.caption_text:
Pregnant and confused about your skincare routine?🤰✨ Comment PREGNANT if you want me to drop a full pregnancy-safe routine or leave your questions below 👀

To be clear: you don’t need to toss everything. Some ingredients are off-limits…but plenty are safe and effective.

And when it comes to sunscreen: everything is a chemical (even water). The best sunscreen is the one you wear every single day. Hope this helps! ❤️

🎥: @melodyymiles on TikTok

items.caption_text:
Cosmetic treatment fails I see far too often and the things you should know before letting anyone near your face.

1️⃣ Skin tag removal
2️⃣ Nose filler
3️⃣ Laser hair removal
4️⃣ CO2 fractional laser

The best advice I can give you is always see a licensed, board-certified professional. Get your shoes on sale… not your face. And groupon deals? Forget about it!

Ask me any and all cosmetic treatment questions below!👇This is my jam and I’m here to help

items.caption_text:
She’s tried Botox. She’s tried great under eye creams. Neither one treats under-eye hollowing. And nobody told her…

At 54, under-eye hollowing is almost always a structural volume problem — fat loss, bone density changes, and sometimes cheek descent depeening the whole area from below.

💉 Botox relaxes muscles
👀 Eye cream sits on the surface
🤷‍♀️ Neither one is reaching the issue

The point is: diagnois first, treatment second. Always. 

Under-eye questions? Let me know below ⬇️ 

✍️Credit to @olha.maikina.md for the facial aging illustration

items.caption_text:
There’s no single miracle treatment…not even a facelift.

A facelift can lift and tighten, but it doesn’t fix skin quality, texture, or uneven tone.

When you expect one procedure to do everything, you’re not just wasting money: you’re missing the bigger picture. 🤷‍♀️ Questions? Ask below 

🎥 blue_eyes_444 on TikTok

P.S. The stupid TikTok beauty filter was on when I recorded this video and I didn’t realize until AFTER filming. All my videos are filter-free so please excuse this mistake 😭

items.caption_text:
Sunlight isn’t the enemy, but unprotected, cumulative UV damage is!

Some of you are confusing the benefits of sunlight with a free pass to skip sun protection… and those are not the same thing.

Wear the damn sunscreen and please don’t subscribe to this stupidity 🤦‍♀️

OG video of sun protected humans (not the stupidity) from @drjenntran

items.caption_text:
🥇Retinol is the gold standard for improving collagen, fine lines, and wrinkles. But the conversation around how often to use it? Not so black and white. 

🤓Here’s the truth:
The data for retinol is based on consistent, long-term use. There are no miracle overnight results.

🚨But if you’re NEW to the retinol game, that does NOT mean you jump in face first because your skin may not be ready to handle it every single night from day one.

The issue? If you go too hard and too fast → you could trigger irritation and inflammation. And irritation doesn’t accelerate results… it sets you back.

💡Retinoids work best when your skin can actually tolerate them.

👩‍⚕️So what does that mean for you?
Starting a few nights a week isn’t “less effective,” and ideally you’re working your way up to using it more nights a week than not. It’s how you build up to effective use without compromising your skin.

⏳ When should you start?
Late 20s / early 30s is great—but honestly, it’s never too late.

🙈 Biggest mistake I see?
Going wrong and strong straight for the strongest option without building tolerance which blows your face up making you give up altogether.

So repeat after me: 
CONSISTENCY OVER INTENSITY. 
Every. Single. Time.

💬 Drop your questions below 💙
And SAVE this for when you’re ready to start (or restart) a retinol.

items.caption_text:
🔥 Hot tip for clearer skin: If you got a little lazy with SPF this winter (it’s okay!) but now is the time to get back on it

Spring is when so many people, including my patients, start noticing their dark spots looking darker 👀 Why? Because even if it’s still cold AF where you live, the UV Index is creeping 🆙

🧴 If the UV Index is 3 or higher, sunscreen is a must.

📲 You can check the UV Index directly in your weather app. Just scroll down. Or, to keep things simple: just wear the damn sunscreen every day 👏

And if dark spots are your main concern, this is the time to get consistent with a targeted routine like the Major Fade Protocol.

Let me know if you have questions below. I’m here to help 🫶

🎥: skin_tia on TikTok
